title: Support to start container with image layer
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Currenlty Podman can start container with rootfs by specify `--rootfs`. The rootfs must be writable. 

Now I have a image layer mount as read-only at somewhere like `/var/lib/containers/test` , I want to start a container which rootfs is overlay on this image layer. Podman can manage container overlay upper/work/merged directory.

How about add some flag like `--root-layer`?

comments.body:
This works for me.  I think you need to add more information.

```
# podman image mount fedora
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9e6713d530bf59dd0ce8155e1a48372e2ad1773be06a8087deafeb5ad0fed586/merged
# mount --bind -o ro /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9e6713d530bf59dd0ce8155e1a48372e2ad1773be06a8087deafeb5ad0fed586/merged /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9e6713d530bf59dd0ce8155e1a48372e2ad1773be06a8087deafeb5ad0fed586/merged
# touch /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9e6713d530bf59dd0ce8155e1a48372e2ad1773be06a8087deafeb5ad0fed586/merged/etc/resolv.conf 
touch: cannot touch '/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9e6713d530bf59dd0ce8155e1a48372e2ad1773be06a8087deafeb5ad0fed586/merged/etc/resolv.conf': Read-only file system
# podman run --read-only --rootfs /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/9e6713d530bf59dd0ce8155e1a48372e2ad1773be06a8087deafeb5ad0fed586/merged echo hi
hi
```

@rhatdan I want to start a writable container with overlayfs. 
The overlayfs lower layer is image layer and has been mounted on somewhere as read-only. And Podman can create writable layer and mount a overley rootfs.
So you want to specify a the upper directory where the overlay can write?
Could you describe the use cases that this would be valuable for, as opposed to you just creating your own overlay and not building it into Podman.

In the docker-container, there is a daemon agent which can mount  a container image to some directory as readonly. I want to reuse this mounted directory to start a nested-container. 

I hope Podman can start the nested-container based on the image mounted directory.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2086945/127796611-49e4b5cd-6c40-4d1c-842a-2039ee302fad.png)

`--rootfs` is backend agnostic as it doesn't have to deal with overlay itself, the user provides the final root for the container.

With the model above, we probably want to implement it in containers/storage to use the overlay driver before it can be exposed in Podman.

I think it is an useful feature.

I would figure something like

podman run --rootfs /:O --tmpfs /var 

Would be kind of cool, allowing you to launch containers on existing file systems that are writable, but, there would be  no way to do a commit on these containers, and lots of stuff would be different then traditional Podman containers.

One issue would be overlay does not like the underlying file system changing from underneath it.

@rhatdan @giuseppe I tried this patch (https://github.com/flouthoc/podman/commit/d66104e6f1e9ece0da23dec36f0fbab0801f57f6) which implements scenarios such as below.
```bash
podman run --rootfs /some/readonly/path:O echo hello
```

I didn't open a PR as i was not really sure if this approach is expected and its crude but i can open a PR if this looks okish.
I think it is fine, as long as the overlay upper layer lives as long as the container (it cannot be on a tmpdir), and that it is reused when the container is restarted
Yes the upper directory should be in containers/storage rootfs just like all other containers uppers.
@rhatdan @giuseppe Thanks for the feedback I will make the requested changes.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Just need to vendor in buildah in a seperate PR.
